region: Northern Thailand (Chiang Mai, Chiang Rai, Mae Hong Son)
textile_history: Hmong textile art includes reverse appliqué (pa ndau), embroidery, and batik. Pa ndau storytelling cloths depict migration history and clan symbols. Hemp was traditionally used before cotton became common.
cultural_significance: Textiles encode clan identity, spiritual beliefs, and historical narratives. The 'snail pattern' represents the Hmong migration journey. New Year textiles showcase the finest work for courtship displays.
